content.problemStatement: Write a program to check whether the given number as input is positive, negative, or zero.
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single integer N.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print "Positive" if N>0, "Negative" if N<0, or "Zero" if N==0, each on its own line.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The first number 1 is positive, the second number -2 is negative, and the third number 0 is zero.
The number -3 is negative and the number 0 is zero.

solution.approach: The problem only requires checking the sign of each integer. For every test case we read N and use a simple if‑else chain: if N>0 output "Positive", else if N<0 output "Negative", otherwise output "Zero". This runs in O(1) per test case and uses O(1) extra memory.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – one constant‑time check per test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few scalar variables are used.
